findings.title: Path-traversal still possible via unvalidated modelFile and commitHash; round-1 fix only covered modelId
findings.description: Round-1 finding #1 was reported as fixed, but the recommendation explicitly called for THREE regex guards (`MODEL_ID_REGEX`, `MODEL_FILE_REGEX`, `COMMIT_HASH_REGEX`) plus an explicit `..` ban. Only `MODEL_ID_REGEX` was added. `modelFile` and `commitHash` remain interpolated into the download URL with zero validation: `https://huggingface.co/$modelId/resolve/$commitHash/$modelFile?download=true` (ModelAllowlist.kt:46). A malicious `modelFile = "../../../attacker-org/evil-repo/resolve/HEAD/weights.bin"` or `commitHash = "../.."` produces a URL that any RFC-3986-compliant client (okhttp, Cloudflare, HF's edge) normalizes to escape the `litert-community` org segment. Net effect: the round-1 critique that TAC-15 host-pinning is only LITERAL-string-pinned, not org-pinned, is unchanged for two of the three trust-boundary inputs. Additionally, the new `require(m.toModel().url.startsWith(URL_PREFIX))` on line 50 is the same tautology as before тАФ `toModel()` always prepends `https://huggingface.co/`, so the assertion can never fail regardless of injected segments.
findings.impact: Same as round-1 finding #1: defense-in-depth hole at the architectural barrier guarding any future Phase 2 OTA / user-imported allowlist code path. Phase 1 ships a trusted bundled JSON so direct exploitability is nil today, but the regression test (parse_rejectsDisallowedModelIdPrefix) gives false confidence that the schema-guard now blocks path traversal тАФ it only blocks ONE of three vectors. A reviewer reading task-4.md and the new tests will reasonably conclude TAC-15 is enforced; it is not.

findings.title: MODEL_ID_REGEX accepts pure dot segments тАФ `..` and `.` collapse the org pin under URL normalization
findings.description: The user's prompt asked to double-check the regex interpretation. The regex `^litert-community/[A-Za-z0-9._-]+$` correctly rejects the round-1 example `litert-community/../attacker` because the slash after the first segment is not in the character class тАФ confirmed. HOWEVER, the character class `[A-Za-z0-9._-]+` accepts a trailing segment composed entirely of dots: `litert-community/..` and `litert-community/...` both match. For `litert-community/..` specifically, the constructed URL is `https://huggingface.co/litert-community/../resolve/COMMIT/FILE?download=true`. RFC 3986 ┬з5.2.4 path-normalization (which okhttp's `HttpUrl` performs eagerly, Cloudflare/HF's edge perform on the wire, and `java.net.URI.normalize()` performs on demand) collapses `litert-community/..` to nothing, yielding `https://huggingface.co/resolve/COMMIT/FILE` тАФ the org segment is gone. While such a URL is unlikely to resolve on HF's actual storage layout (returns 404), the regex no longer enforces the org-pin invariant claimed by TAC-15. A more interesting variant: `litert-community/..%2F..` is rejected because `%` is not in the class тАФ good тАФ but a future contributor weakening the class (adding `~` or `%`) would silently re-open the door. The regex needs a stricter shape that excludes pure dot segments.
findings.impact: Defense-in-depth gap. Combined with finding #1 above (unvalidated modelFile/commitHash), an attacker who controls allowlist content in a hypothetical Phase 2 path can construct URLs whose normalized form bypasses the litert-community org pin. Within Phase 1 тАФ no exploitability; the bundled JSON is trusted. Severity downgraded to major (not critical) because the trailing dot-segment variant alone, without finding #1, only escapes ONE path component and points at HF subpaths that don't host model files; it is finding #1's combination with this that produces a working bypass.

findings.title: fixtureMatchesProductionAsset depends on Gradle module CWD тАФ fragile under non-Gradle test runners
findings.description: The new test `fixtureMatchesProductionAsset` uses `File("src/main/assets/model_allowlist.json")` and `File("src/test/resources/model_allowlist_fixture.json")` (relative paths). This works under `./gradlew :core-runtime:test` because Gradle sets CWD to the module directory by default. It silently NO-OPS (or misreports) under: IntelliJ Run Configurations with `Working directory = $MODULE_WORKING_DIR$` set to project root, Bazel/Buck wrapper builds, GitHub Actions matrix jobs that set `working-directory` at the workflow level. The first `assertTrue("prod asset missing: ...", prod.exists())` does fail-loud rather than silently pass, which mitigates the issue тАФ credit for that. Still, classpath-relative resolution is portable; CWD-relative is not.
findings.impact: If the test ever silently fails to find the prod asset (e.g., a future GHA job adds `working-directory: ./` at the root), the drift guard becomes a no-op without alerting reviewers. The current fail-loud `assertTrue(prod.exists())` reduces the risk to operational (not security).
findings.recommendation: Either resolve via Gradle-injected system property (`System.getProperty("prodAssetPath")` set in `core-runtime/build.gradle.kts` test block), or copy the prod asset into test resources via a Gradle `processTestResources` task and read both via `getResourceAsStream`. Lower-effort alternative: assert `prod.absolutePath` ends with the expected suffix to fail loudly if CWD is wrong. Defer to a follow-up тАФ current implementation is fail-loud and acceptable for Phase 1.

findings.title: Per-entry required-field NPE messages still uninformative (round-1 finding #4 carry-over)
findings.description: Round-1 finding #4 was deferred. The diff confirms it: `models` got an explicit `!models.isNullOrEmpty()` check with a clear error message and a comment explaining Gson's null-bypass behavior, but per-entry required fields (`modelId`, `modelFile`, `commitHash`, `name`, `sizeInBytes`, `taskTypes`) still rely on Kotlin's compile-time non-null types that Gson populates with null at runtime. A JSON entry missing `"modelId"` will throw `NullPointerException` on line 44 `MODEL_ID_REGEX.matches(m.modelId)` before the `require` lambda formats its message. Still fail-closed (`runCatching` wraps it) тАФ no security-state escape тАФ but ErrorLog (Task 5) records a bare `NullPointerException` rather than `"modelId missing in allowlist entry #N"`. Acknowledged as deferred in the round-1 review; re-flagged here for tracking, no severity change.
findings.impact: Operational/diagnostic only. A09 (Security Logging and Monitoring) is weaker than necessary for Phase 2 OTA scenarios where allowlist authors are not the same humans who read the crash logs.
findings.recommendation: Same as round-1: add `require(!m.modelId.isNullOrBlank())` etc. before the regex guards. Defer if not landed in this task; track for Task 5 or a Phase 2 hardening pass.
